source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: Papers dedicated to Florin Gheorghe Filip, Fellow of the Romanian Academy (Editorial)
paper.abstract: Editorial introducing papers dedicated to Prof. Florin Gheorghe Filip at his 75th anniversary and completion of 15 years as President of the Information Science and Technology Section of the Romanian Academy.

verdict.summary: Editorial content introducing a special issue. No technical claims requiring formalism analysis. Excluded from scoring.

papers.provenance:

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Editorial introduction
**Level:** N/A (Editorial)
**Rationale:** No technical content requiring formalism.

### Necessity Test
N/A - Editorial content.

**Verdict:** Editorial excluded from analysis.

### Issues
None - not applicable.

paper.title: Infinite Spike Trains in Spiking Neural P Systems
paper.abstract: Study of spiking neural P systems as computability devices generating infinite sequences of bits and as devices processing infinite sequences. Establishes basic properties and formulates research topics on computing Boolean operations and morphisms on infinite bit sequences.

verdict.summary: High-quality theoretical computer science paper on membrane computing. All formalism is necessary: SN P system definitions (Def. 1), regular expressions over alphabet V, firing/forgetting rules with rigorous syntax (E/a^c -> a; d). Level 5 theoretical complexity requires and justifies heavy formalism. Authors include Rozenberg (Leiden) - foundational figures in formal language theory.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Computational theory - membrane computing, infinite sequences
**Level:** 5 (Theoretical Computer Science / Computability Theory)
**Rationale:** Extends computability theory to infinite sequences using SN P systems. Novel theoretical contributions on Turing universality and morphism computation.

### Necessity Test
- SN P system formal definition (sigma = (O, sigma_1,...,sigma_m, syn, out)): NECESSARY - standard membrane computing formalism
- Regular expression definitions: NECESSARY - required for rule specification
- Firing rules E/a^c -> a; d notation: NECESSARY - standard for membrane computing
- Binary spike train definitions: NECESSARY - core contribution
- Kolmogorov complexity references: NECESSARY - relates to computability bounds

**Verdict:** All formalism necessary. This is foundational theoretical CS from leading researchers (Paun - founder of membrane computing, Rozenberg - formal language theory pioneer).

### Issues
None identified. Exemplary theoretical paper.

paper.title: Approach to Evaluate the Data of Moss Biomonitoring Studies: Preprocessing and Preliminary Ranking
paper.abstract: Approach to describe biomonitoring data using mosses in Republic of Moldova. Correlation analysis with ranking, data smoothing using discrete cosine transform, and determination of linearly independent basis vectors for matrix decomposition.

verdict.summary: Applied data analysis for environmental monitoring. Formalism includes correlation analysis equations, DCT smoothing (Eq. 1-3), covariance matrix formulation. Level 2-3 applied statistics work. Formalism is standard and necessary for the methodology described.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Environmental data preprocessing and statistical analysis
**Level:** 2-3 (Applied Statistics / Data Analysis)
**Rationale:** Standard statistical methods applied to environmental monitoring data.

### Necessity Test
- Correlation coefficient formulas: NECESSARY - core methodology
- RSS + penalty term objective function (Eq. 1): NECESSARY - DCT smoothing basis
- Tridiagonal matrix D (Eq. 3): NECESSARY - finite difference operator
- Principal component extraction: NECESSARY - dimensionality reduction

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for applied statistics paper. No decorative elements.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Meaning in Action: a Qualitative Approach of Information
paper.abstract: Qualitative exploration of the concept of information. Reviews Shannon's information theory, Kolmogorov-Chaitin algorithmic information theory, and Tononi's integrated information theory. Proposes qualitative definition: information as symbolic structure that acts through meaning in context.

verdict.summary: Philosophical/theoretical paper on information theory foundations. Reviews Shannon entropy I(E) = -sum(p_i log p_i), Kolmogorov complexity K_f(x|y), Chaitin complexity C_M(x). Level 4 theoretical work. All formalism cited from foundational sources and necessary for the comparative analysis.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Philosophy of information / Information theory foundations
**Level:** 4 (Theoretical / Philosophical)
**Rationale:** Comparative analysis of foundational information theories, proposing qualitative framework.

### Necessity Test
- Shannon entropy formula I(E) = -sum(p_i log p_i): NECESSARY - reviewing Shannon's theory
- Kolmogorov complexity K_f(x|y) (Def. 1): NECESSARY - explaining algorithmic information
- Chaitin complexity C_M(x) (Def. 2): NECESSARY - explaining program complexity
- Algorithmic entropy H(s) (Def. 4): NECESSARY - comparative analysis
- Draganescu's generalized information N = <S, M> (Def. 6): NECESSARY - qualitative framework

**Verdict:** All formalism necessary - this is a review/synthesis paper requiring precise definitions from multiple theories.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: AHB Interconnect - Functional Verification
paper.abstract: Verification procedure for AHB Interconnect module integration. Functional verification using simulation-based testing with UVM methodology. Environment includes Master/Slave agents, Scoreboard, and Coverage Collectors.

verdict.summary: Engineering paper on hardware verification methodology. Level 2 applied engineering. Minimal formalism - mostly block diagrams (Fig. 1-3) and protocol descriptions. No unnecessary mathematical formalism. Appropriate for the practical verification engineering domain.

papers.provenance:

**Topic:** Hardware verification / IC design verification

**Rationale:** Practical verification environment for AHB protocol compliance.

### Necessity Test
- UVM methodology description: NECESSARY - standard verification approach
- Block diagrams (Fig. 1-3): NECESSARY - architecture illustration
- Protocol rules (SINGLE, INCR, WRAP types): NECESSARY - verification requirements
- No unnecessary mathematical formalism present

**Verdict:** Appropriate minimal formalism for engineering verification paper.

paper.title: Twitter's Mirroring of the 2022 Energy Crisis: What It Teaches Decision-Makers - A Preliminary Study
paper.abstract: Opinion mining on Twitter during 2022 energy crisis. Analysis of socio-ethical and ICT-related concerns. Uses lexical, topical, and semantical analysis with coherence indices. Determines association probabilities between ethical, socio-economic, and ICT concerns.

verdict.summary: Applied NLP/social media analysis. Level 2 empirical study. Formalism includes topic coherence formula C(t, V(t)) (Eq. 1) from Mimno et al., appropriate for NLP methodology. Standard statistical analysis for social media research.

papers.provenance:

**Level:** 2 (Applied NLP / Empirical Study)
**Rationale:** Standard NLP techniques applied to Twitter data.

### Necessity Test
- Topic coherence formula C(t, V(t)) (Eq. 1): NECESSARY - established NLP metric from Mimno et al.
- Adherence definition A(t1, V(t1); t2, U(t2)): NECESSARY - novel adaptation for inter-topic analysis
- Descriptive statistics (Table 1): NECESSARY - data characterization

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for NLP methodology paper.

None identified.

paper.title: XOR-Based Detector of Different Decisions on Anomalies in the Computer Network Traffic
paper.abstract: Anomaly-based IDS using ML classifiers (wk-NN, DT, FNN, SVM). Novel hyperbolic tangent feature scaling based on Levenberg-Marquardt linearization. XOR-based detector for conflicting decisions between parallel classifiers on Kyoto 2006+ dataset.

verdict.summary: Applied ML for network security. Level 2-3 applied ML. Formalism includes feature scaling equations, Levenberg-Marquardt damping strategy, XOR fusion logic. Standard ML methodology presentation. No decorative formalism.

papers.provenance:

- Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm (pseudo-code): NECESSARY - explains damping strategy

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for applied ML paper.

paper.title: A Data Hiding Approach for Secured Data Communication
paper.abstract: Prime Number Based Embedding (PNBE) steganography approach. Novel scheme for identifying embedding location using prime numbers. Encoding/decoding algorithms for data hiding in images without visual distortion.

verdict.summary: Applied steganography paper. Level 2 engineering. Algorithm 1 (PNBE encoding) and Algorithm 2 (PNBE decoding) are presented as pseudo-code. Image grouping scheme (Fig. 2) is visual. Formalism is minimal and appropriate - uses modular arithmetic for prime-based embedding logic.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Steganography / Data hiding
**Level:** 2 (Applied Information Security)
**Rationale:** Novel embedding scheme using prime number properties.

### Necessity Test
- Algorithm 1 (PNBE encoding): NECESSARY - core contribution
- Algorithm 2 (PNBE decoding): NECESSARY - complementary operation
- Modular arithmetic (G % E): NECESSARY - embedding logic
- Image grouping diagram (Fig. 2): NECESSARY - explains spatial embedding

**Verdict:** Minimal formalism appropriate for algorithmic contribution.

### Issues

paper.title: Binary Anarchic Society Optimization for Feature Selection
paper.abstract: Binary version of Anarchic Society Optimization (BASO) for feature selection. Human-inspired metaheuristic algorithm using fickleness, external irregularity, and internal irregularity indices. Evaluated on 9 UCI datasets against BGA, BPSO, BGWO.

verdict.summary: Metaheuristic optimization for feature selection. Level 2-3 applied optimization. Formalism includes Fickleness Index FI_i(k) (Eq. 1), External Irregularity Index EI_i(k) (Eq. 2) - standard for swarm intelligence algorithms. Movement policy equations are necessary for algorithm specification.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Metaheuristic optimization / Feature selection
**Level:** 2-3 (Applied Optimization)
**Rationale:** Binary adaptation of ASO algorithm for wrapper-based feature selection.

### Necessity Test
- Fickleness Index FI_i(k) = (f(X_i(k)) - f(X_alpha(k)(k))) / f(X_alpha(k)(k)) (Eq. 1): NECESSARY - ASO component
- External Irregularity Index EI_i(k) (Eq. 2): NECESSARY - ASO component
- Movement policies MP_Current, MP_Society, MP_Past: NECESSARY - algorithm mechanics
- Transfer function for binarization: NECESSARY - binary adaptation

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for metaheuristic algorithm paper.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Using Swear Words Increases the Irritability - a Study Using AI Algorithms
paper.abstract: Analysis of swearing effects on irritability using CNN for speech emotion recognition. 28 volunteers, two groups (swearing vs. non-swearing). CNN with MFCCs (87,944 parameters) detects 8 emotion classes. Results: 14% increase in negative emotion, 21% increase in irritability for swearing group.

verdict.summary: Applied psychology study using CNN for emotion detection. Level 2 applied ML/psychology. Minimal formalism - CNN architecture described textually (87,944 parameters, ReLU, dropout 20%). Results presented as percentages and histograms. Appropriate for interdisciplinary study.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Psychology / Emotion recognition with ML
**Level:** 2 (Applied ML for Psychology)
**Rationale:** Interdisciplinary study combining psychological questionnaires with CNN-based speech analysis.

### Necessity Test
- CNN architecture description: NECESSARY - methodology specification
- MFCC feature extraction: NECESSARY - standard audio processing
- Statistical results (14%, 21%): NECESSARY - findings presentation
- Histograms (Fig. 3-4): NECESSARY - distribution visualization

**Verdict:** Minimal formalism appropriate for interdisciplinary empirical study.

### Issues
Small sample size (n=28) limits statistical power, but this is a methodological concern not a formalism issue.

paper.title: Speech Emotion Recognition Using Deep Neural Networks, Transfer Learning, and Ensemble Classification Techniques
paper.abstract: SER systems using DNNs with five complexity levels: single DNNs, ensemble classifiers (OvO, OvR), transfer learning with image recognition models (Xception, VGG, ResNet, EfficientNet), heterogeneous and homogeneous TL-DNN ensembles. Tested on EMODB, CREMAD, IEMOCAP.

verdict.summary: Comprehensive ML/DL paper on speech emotion recognition. Level 3 applied ML research. Formalism includes architecture diagrams (Fig. 1-5), TL-DNN model comparison table (Table 1), OvO/OvR ensemble formulations. State-of-the-art results on EMODB (83% accuracy). Well-structured experimental methodology.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Speech emotion recognition / Deep learning
**Level:** 3 (Applied Deep Learning Research)
**Rationale:** Systematic comparison of five DNN approaches with transfer learning.

### Necessity Test
- Architecture diagrams (Fig. 1-5): NECESSARY - system illustration
- TL-DNN comparison table (Table 1): NECESSARY - model selection justification
- OvO ensemble: K(K-1)/2 classifiers: NECESSARY - methodology
- OvR ensemble: K classifiers: NECESSARY - methodology
- Dataset specifications (EMODB-7, CREMAD-6, IEMOCAP-4): NECESSARY - experimental setup
- Metrics (UA, WA, P, R, F1, rho, rho_c): NECESSARY - evaluation

**Verdict:** Comprehensive and appropriate formalism for deep learning research paper.

### Issues
None identified.

batch_notes: Volume 26 Issue 3-4 (2023) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. This is a special issue dedicated to Acad. Florin Gheorghe Filip. **No formalism theater detected.** Papers span theoretical CS (membrane computing), applied ML/DL, data analysis, information theory, and engineering verification. Notable: Paper 748 (Paun et al.) is exemplary theoretical work from leading membrane computing researchers. All formalism across papers is domain-appropriate and necessary. Final: 0 CRITICAL, 0 LIMITED, 7 ADEQUATE, 3 STRONG, 1 EDITORIAL.
